2007-02-01.title: Palestinian Shootout Kills 6 as Truce Breaks Down
2007-02-01.abstract: Members of Hamas today fired on a convoy from the rival Fatah faction, touching off an intense gun battle.

2007-02-01.title: Legislature Grants Chávez Broad New Powers to Shape Venezuela
2007-02-01.abstract: Venezuela’s legislature, in which every member is already a supporter of President Hugo Chávez, gave him the power to rule by decree for 18 months.

2007-02-01.title: Malawi: Vice President’s Treason Trial Begins
2007-02-01.abstract: Malawi’s tumultuous politics crossed another threshold as the vice president, Cassim Chilumpha, went on trial for treason.

2007-02-01.title: Berlusconi Flirts. Wife’s Fed Up. Read All About It.
2007-02-01.abstract: The wife of Italy’s former prime minister asked her husband to apologize — in a front-page letter to the editor.

2007-02-01.title: Hong Kong Democracy Advocate Says He Faces Long Odds in Vote
2007-02-01.abstract: A candidate for chief executive said he had no chance of beating the Beijing-backed incumbent.

2007-02-02.title: In Uproar, Chirac Again Retracts Remarks on Nuclear Iran
2007-02-02.abstract: President Jacques Chirac reaffirmed that France remained committed to preventing Iran from becoming a nuclear power.

2007-02-02.title: Chinese Official Publishes Rebuke of Bush
2007-02-02.abstract: The rebuke accused President Bush of waging a “unilateral” battle against terrorists that had worsened global tensions.

2007-02-02.title: Venezuela: Chávez Attacks Bush, Continued
2007-02-02.abstract: President Hugo Chávez said President Bush should be tried for “war crimes” in relation to the Iraq war. He showed further disdain for Mr. Bush, saying he was unfit to manage “even a Little League baseball team,” in reaction to Mr. Bush’s publicized concern about Mr. Chávez’s new power to rule by decree. “They should be tried and thrown in prison for the rest of their days,” Mr. Chávez said of Mr. Bush and John D. Negroponte, who is designated for the No. 2 position in the State Department. Mr. Chávez also called Mr. Bush “the devil,” as he did in a speech at the United Nations in September.

2007-02-02.title: U.S. Dialogue With China Suffers Setback
2007-02-02.abstract: A top official in charge of the China dialogue resigned as talks on a key trade dispute failed to resolve differences.

2007-02-02.title: Canada: Five Dead in 20-Vehicle Crash
2007-02-02.abstract: Five people were killed in a multi-vehicle pileup east of Toronto that brought traffic on Canada’s busiest route, Highway 401, to a standstill. As heavy snow fell and people worked to rescue victims trapped in cars, a tanker truck, one of at least 20 vehicles involved in the accident, burst into flames, producing a plume of black smoke that marked the site for hundreds of cars and trucks backed up for miles in both directions.

2007-02-03.title: Bomb Resonates With Diplomats, Not With the Bomber
2007-02-03.abstract: There are not many places where a man convicted in the bombing of a commercial airliner can be found roaming the streets. Caracas, Venezuela is one of them.

2007-02-03.title: Republicans Plan to Block Iraq Debate
2007-02-03.abstract: The Democratic majority leader responded that the move was essentially filibustering a debate of Iraq policy to avoid a judgment on the president.

2007-02-03.title: Factional Violence Continues in Gaza
2007-02-03.abstract: Forces loyal to Hamas appeared to consolidate their hold on northern Gaza and much of Gaza City, with 12 people wounded in clashes with Fatah.

2007-02-03.title: 2 Killed as U.S. Helicopter Is Shot Down Near Baghdad
2007-02-03.abstract: This was the fourth American helicopter to be shot down or crash under fire in the last two weeks.

2007-02-03.title: Iran Installs Equipment for Large-Scale Uranium Enrichment
2007-02-03.abstract: The move signals a continued defiance of a United Nations Security Council resolution requiring Iran to suspend its nuclear activities.

2007-02-04.title: With Rumsfeld Gone, Critics of War Look to Rice
2007-02-04.abstract: In recent weeks the secretary of state has faced increased, and somewhat unfamiliar, criticism.

2007-02-04.title: Deadly Bird Flu Confirmed in British Turkeys
2007-02-04.abstract: An outbreak of bird flu at a poultry farm was caused by a strain that has killed humans, the authorities said.

2007-02-04.title: On Road to Kosovo Independence, a Warning: Go Slow
2007-02-04.abstract: As the U.N. nudges Kosovo toward what it calls “final status” and what Belgrade calls independence, many of northern Kosovo’s Serbs are threatening to break away.

2007-02-04.title: Iraqis Fault Delayed U.S. Plan in Attack
2007-02-04.abstract: A growing number of Iraqis blamed the United States for creating conditions that led to the single worst suicide bombing in the war.

2007-02-04.title: At Least 130 Die as Blast Levels Baghdad Market
2007-02-04.abstract: The unrelenting killing of Shiites promises to put more pressure on Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki of Iraq.

2007-02-05.title: New Charges Against Imprisoned Yukos Founder
2007-02-05.abstract: The charges opened a new line of legal attack against a Kremlin foe who was once regarded as Russia’s richest man.

2007-02-05.title: Senior Iraqi Urges Iran to Hold Talks With U.S.
2007-02-05.abstract: TEHRAN, Feb. 5—A senior Iraqi Shiite leader said Monday at a news conference in Tehran that his country was urging Iran to hold talks with the United States and that such talks are important for the security of the region.

2007-02-05.title: Former Cabinet Officials Arrested in Bangladesh Corruption Sweep
2007-02-05.abstract: The arrests followed a promise by the interim government to cleanse politics of organized crime and corruption.

2007-02-05.title: AIDS Advocate Under House Arrest in China
2007-02-05.abstract: The arrest prevented her from traveling to an awards ceremony in the United States held by a nonprofit group connected to Sen. Hillary Rodham Clinton.

2007-02-05.title: Car Bombs Kill Dozens in Baghdad
2007-02-05.abstract: The bombs, which killed at least 25 people, came as Iraqi and American forces prepare to step up the latest attempt to secure the city.

2007-02-06.title: Iranian Diplomat Kidnapped in Baghdad by Iraqis With Official ID
2007-02-06.abstract: Serious questions were raised about whether Iraqi government forces were involved in the abduction.

2007-02-06.title: Lawmaker in Iraq Convicted for Embassy Blasts in ‘83
2007-02-06.abstract: Officials confirmed that an Iraqi lawmaker is the same man who was convicted of planning bombing attacks on the U.S. and French embassies in Kuwait.

2007-02-06.title: Indian Army and Police Tied to Kashmir Killings
2007-02-06.abstract: A string of recent deaths has unearthed a deep well of resentment among the people of Indian-administered Kashmir.

2007-02-06.title: Drawing Lines Across the Sand, Between Classes
2007-02-06.abstract: In Brazil, some bodies — and some beaches — are more equal than others.

2007-02-06.title: Germany Agonizes Over a Brain Drain
2007-02-06.abstract: The steady exodus in recent years has become a hot topic in a land already saddled with one of the most rapidly aging and shrinking populations of any Western nation.

2007-02-07.title: Pipeline Company Settles Charges Involving Iraq
2007-02-07.abstract: UNITED NATIONS, Feb. 7 — The El Paso Corporation, the largest American natural gas pipeline operator, agreed today to pay the federal government $7.73 million to settle allegations that it was involved in illegal payments under the United Nations-run oil-for-food program in Iraq.

2007-02-07.title: Iraqi Premier Admits Errors in Introducing Security Plan in Baghdad
2007-02-07.abstract: The prime minister acknowledged that his government had stumbled in its efforts to carry out a new security plan in Baghdad but offered no reasons for the delay.

2007-02-07.title: Indonesians Still Seeking High Ground
2007-02-07.abstract: With more than 220,000 people still homeless, some evacuees congregated on the high ground of a cemetery.

2007-02-07.title: Seven Killed as U.S. Copter Crashes in Iraq
2007-02-07.abstract: The crash of a military helicopter was at least the fifth in recent days, as the long-awaited Baghdad security operation got formally under way.

2007-02-07.title: U.S. to Create a Single Command for Military Operations in Africa
2007-02-07.abstract: The centralized command will replace three posts that now divide responsibility for operations in Africa.

2007-02-08.title: Noted Arab Citizens Call on Israel to Shed Jewish Identity
2007-02-08.abstract: A group of Israeli Arabs has said Israel should become a “consensual democracy for both Arabs and Jews,” prompting consternation and debate.

2007-02-08.title: Palestinians Reach Deal on Government
2007-02-08.abstract: The two main Palestinian factions, Hamas and Fatah, agreed today to form a unity government and settle some of the disputes that have led to months of violence between them.

2007-02-08.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Lashes Out Against U.S.
2007-02-08.abstract: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei warned the U.S. that Iran would respond to any aggression by striking back worldwide.

2007-02-08.title: Chirac Hints He Won’t Run for Third Term
2007-02-08.abstract: Officials played down the significance of President Jacques Chirac’s comments, saying they were of a general nature.

2007-02-08.title: Copter Crashes Suggest Shift in Iraqi Tactics
2007-02-08.abstract: American officials say a streak of crashes strongly suggests that insurgents have changed tactics and are putting more effort into shooting down aircraft.

2007-02-09.title: Dogfighting in Russia
2007-02-09.abstract: In the former Soviet Union, dogfighting competitions have surged in popularity since the collapse of Communism.

2007-02-09.title: France: French Doctor to Lead Global Disease Fund
2007-02-09.abstract: Dr. Michel Kazatchkine, the top official in France’s battle against AIDS, was chosen as the new chief of the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria, the organization announced in Geneva. He succeeds Dr. Richard G. A. Feachem. The fund has disbursed $3.4 billion since 2001, provided AIDS treatment to more than 500,000 people in 127 countries and handed out 11 million mosquito nets to fight malaria. Dr. Kazatchkine has treated people with AIDS for more than 20 years.

2007-02-09.title: Civic Groups Seek to Amend Law on Insults to Turkish State
2007-02-09.abstract: Civic organizations proposed changes to the section of the Turkish penal code that makes insulting Turkey or Turkishness a crime.

2007-02-09.title: Five Face Terrorism Charges in British Court
2007-02-09.abstract: The suspects were seized in connection with what the police called a conspiracy to capture and kill a British soldier.

2007-02-09.title: A Real-Life Mystery: The Hunt for the Lost Leonardo
2007-02-09.abstract: An art detective is poised to solve the mystery of what happened to “The Battle of Anghiari,” considered by some experts to be Leonardo’s greatest painting.

2007-02-10.title: Inviting Africa’s Anglicans to Gather Under a Bigger Tent
2007-02-10.abstract: A global meeting of Anglican leaders in Tanzania will focus on the issue of homosexuality, which currently threatens to break the church apart.

2007-02-10.title: Peru: President Questioned in 1986 Massacre
2007-02-10.abstract: President Alan García was questioned by a judge about his actions during the killing of more than 100 suspected Maoist guerrillas during his first presidency in the 1980s. Peru’s Congress declined to bring criminal charges for the deaths against Mr. García in 1990 for the incident, in which Peruvian soldiers suppressed a rebellion at El Frontón prison in 1986 and then transferred the bodies of prisoners to anonymous graves on Lima’s outskirts. Reuters reported that Mr. García was not a target of the judge’s investigation. The questioning of Mr. García raises uneasy memories of his first term, which was haunted by political and economic chaos.

2007-02-10.title: Britain: Man Detained in Letter Bombing
2007-02-10.abstract: A 48-year-old man called a BBC radio phone-in show to take responsibility for sending one of seven letter bombs that exploded in British offices in recent weeks. The station did not permit him on air. The man, whose name was not made public, was later detained under the Mental Health Act. The police are investigating if the bombs, which have not caused serious injuries, were sent by animal rights protesters or by people angered by restrictions on motorists.

2007-02-10.title: World Atomic Agency Suspends 22 Programs of Aid to Iran
2007-02-10.abstract: Washington objected to the programs and projects as aiding Tehran’s nuclear efforts.

2007-02-10.title: Inquiry on Intelligence Gaps May Reach to White House
2007-02-10.abstract: Current and former White House aides will be asked to testify about a report that criticizes the Pentagon for compiling “alternative intelligence” to make the case for invading Iraq.

2007-02-11.title: Taliban Stepping Up Attacks in Afghanistan
2007-02-11.abstract: Attacks by Taliban insurgents in southern Afghanistan have increased in recent days in what seems to be the opening of the fighting season, officials said Saturday.

2007-02-11.title: Portugal to Vote on Putting End to Abortion Ban
2007-02-11.abstract: A referendum on loosening restrictive abortion laws has pitted the Socialist government against Roman Catholics.

2007-02-11.title: Putin Says U.S. Is Undermining Global Stability
2007-02-11.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia accused the United States of provoking a new nuclear arms race.

2007-02-11.title: Turkmenistan Holds Carefully Managed Election
2007-02-11.abstract: The arefully choreographed vote was almost certain to be won by a confidante of the reclusive nation’s former autocratic leader, who died seven weeks ago.

2007-02-11.title: Spain Says Adiós Siesta and Hola Viagra
2007-02-11.abstract: In Spain’s Viagra explosion, the drug is as popular with teenage clubbers as it is with men in their 70s.

2007-02-12.title: Low Turnout Undercuts Portugal Vote on Abortion
2007-02-12.abstract: A referendum to liberalize the abortion law was approved, but turnout was too low for the result to be deemed valid.

2007-02-12.title: Draft Accord Reached in North Korea Nuclear Talks
2007-02-12.abstract: Negotiators were awaiting approval of a draft accord on North Korean nuclear disarmament from their respective governments, the chief U.S. envoy said.

2007-02-12.title: Market Attacks in Baghdad  Kill at Least 67
2007-02-12.abstract: The attacks, which came on the anniversary of the bombing of a Shiite shrine, seemed designed to fuel the country’s sectarian hatreds.

2007-02-12.title: Little Doubt on Result of Turkmenistan Vote
2007-02-12.abstract: The vote is almost certain to be won by a confidant of the Central Asian nation’s former autocratic leader, who died seven weeks ago.

2007-02-12.title: German Court Orders Release of Far-Left Terrorist
2007-02-12.abstract: Brigitte Mohnhaupt of the far-left terrorist Red Army Faction has spent 24 years in prison for carrying out several kidnappings and murders in the 1970s.

2007-02-13.title: Two Markets Bombed in Central Baghdad, Killing at Least 67 and Wounding 155
2007-02-13.abstract: Monday’s attacks seemed intended to fuel the country’s sectarian hatred and upstage the new American-Iraqi security plan for Baghdad.

2007-02-13.title: In Shift, Accord on North Korea Seems to Be Set
2007-02-13.abstract: The tentative agreement calls for North Korea to start disabling its nuclear facilities and to allow inspectors back in exchange for fuel oil and aid.

2007-02-13.title: Vaquita Porpoise, and a Way of Life, Face Extinction
2007-02-13.abstract: At the tip of the Gulf of California, the fate of a small, endangered porpoise hangs in the balance.

2007-02-13.title: Taiwan Opposition Leader Faces Corruption Charges
2007-02-13.abstract: Charges were filed today against Ma Ying-jeou, who has been seen as a leading presidential candidate in 2008.

2007-02-13.title: European Officials Agree to Widen Economic Sanctions Against Iran Over Nuclear Program
2007-02-13.abstract: European officials said a resolution negotiated over the last week should go far toward satisfying the Bush administration.

2007-02-14.title: Outside Pressures Broke Korean Deadlock
2007-02-14.abstract: Both beset by huge problems, George W. Bush and Kim Jong-il needed some kind of breakthrough.

2007-02-14.title: Iraqis Announce New Crackdown Across Baghdad
2007-02-14.abstract: The plan is a surprising and highly challenging new effort to reverse the tide of sectarian cleansing that has left the capital bloodied and Balkanized.

2007-02-14.title: Disputes Emerge on Iran and Roadside Bombs
2007-02-14.abstract: The disagreements have laid bare a tension: how and when to draw firm conclusions from battlefield intelligence about the motivations of foreign leaders.

2007-02-14.title: Bomb Kills 11 on Military Bus in Iran
2007-02-14.abstract: An ethnic Baluchi opposition group claimed responsibility for the attack today, an Iranian news agency said.

2007-02-14.title: U.S. Says Powerful Iraqi Cleric Is Living in Iran
2007-02-14.abstract: Officials suggested that the Shiite cleric Moktada al-Sadr might have fled Iraq to avoid being captured or killed during the U.S. crackdown.

2007-02-15.title: The Two Haitis
2007-02-15.abstract: There are two Haitis today: one where tourists play and another that is plagued by poverty and violence.

2007-02-15.title: Russian Trial on Journalist’s Slaying Opens
2007-02-15.abstract: The retrial of three men charged with murdering Paul Klebnikov, the American editor of the Russian-language edition of Forbes magazine here, opened in confusion today as the two suspects failed to appear in court.

2007-02-15.title: Iraq War Planner Was Hired by Military University, Briefly
2007-02-15.abstract: Last June, Douglas J. Feith, a former top Pentagon official, was hired by a Defense Department graduate school with a four-year contract that was to pay him half a million dollars, Pentagon records show.

2007-02-15.title: Oil Company to Pay $200 Million in Toxic Dumping in Ivory Coast
2007-02-15.abstract: Trafigura, a company that had been accused of illegally dumping petrochemical waste, said the payment was not an admission of fault.

2007-02-15.title: Senate Plans to Vote Again on War Resolution
2007-02-15.abstract: The Senate will convene in an unusual Saturday session to vote on the same resolution under debate in the House.

2007-02-16.title: Estonia: President Rejects Vote to Remove Soviet Monuments
2007-02-16.abstract: President Toomas Hendrik Ilves said he would veto legislation to authorize the removal of Soviet war monuments, specifically a memorial in the capital, Tallinn, known as the Bronze Soldier. The monument, viewed by many in Estonia as a symbol of Soviet occupation after World War II, has become the focus of intense protests in Estonia and had prompted furious denunciations from Russia. Parliament had narrowly approved the legislation to relocate the Bronze Soldier in a third and final vote earlier in the day, but in a statement Mr. Ilves called aspects of the legislation unconstitutional, though he did not elaborate.

2007-02-16.title: Dispute Over Iraqi Cleric, Said to Have Gone to Iran
2007-02-16.abstract: Two Shiite leaders took exception with suggestions that Moktada al-Sadr had fled because of a crackdown on militias.

2007-02-16.title: Rice Is Said to Have Speeded North Korea Deal
2007-02-16.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice bypassed layers of government policy review that had derailed past efforts to negotiate an agreement.

2007-02-16.title: Rice Faces an Uphill Battle for a Mideast Breakthrough
2007-02-16.abstract: Prospects for any real progress on Arab-Israeli peace are bleak, especially since a national unity government of Fatah and Hamas leaders emerged.

2007-02-16.title: Pressing Allies, President Warns of Afghan Battle
2007-02-16.abstract: President Bush on Thursday pressed NATO allies to provide a bigger and more aggressive force to protect the fragile Afghan state.

2007-02-17.title: Indonesia Offering Samples of Bird Flu for Vaccines
2007-02-17.abstract: Indonesia will resume sending avian flu virus samples to the World Health Organization as soon as it is guaranteed access to affordable vaccines against the disease, the country announced.

2007-02-17.title: Mexico: U.S. Security Chief in First Visit
2007-02-17.abstract: On his first official visit to Mexico, Homeland Security Secretary Michael Chertoff said he considered an expanded guest-worker program for Mexican migrants as key to stopping terrorists and criminals from slipping into the United States. “Every time a Border Patrol officer is transporting a load of future housekeepers and landscapers to some place to be returned, he’s not looking for drug dealers or drug loads,” Mr. Chertoff said after two days of meetings with Mexico’s top security officials.

2007-02-17.title: Calm Prevails at Holy Site in Jerusalem
2007-02-17.abstract: Despite calls by Muslim religious figures for mass protests, Jerusalem’s Old City was largely calm on Friday.

2007-02-17.title: Bomb Kills Doctor in Pakistani Border Area
2007-02-17.abstract: The attack was another indication of a creeping militancy in Pakistan’s border regions.

2007-02-17.title: A Divided House Denounces Plan for More Troops
2007-02-17.abstract: The resolution was approved 246 to 182. The Senate is considering today whether to move ahead toward a vote on an Iraq resolution.

2007-02-18.title: Suicide Bomber Kills Judge and 14 Others in Pakistani Courtroom
2007-02-18.abstract: In the sixth suicide bombing in a month, a man blew himself up in a small district court in the border town of Quetta on Saturday.

2007-02-18.title: Car Bombs Kill 60 at Market in Baghdad
2007-02-18.abstract: The blasts occurred in a mostly Shiite neighborhood only minutes after American soldiers passed through the area on patrol.

2007-02-18.title: Planning Seen in Iraqi Attacks on U.S. Copters
2007-02-18.abstract: Documents captured from insurgents indicate that some of the recent attacks are the result of a strategy to down coalition aircraft.

2007-02-18.title: A Film Star in Kampala, Conjuring Amin’s Ghost
2007-02-18.abstract: “The Last King of Scotland” depicts an era Ugandans like to forget, but they are proud it is about their country.

2007-02-18.title: Jailed 2 Years, Iraqi Tells of Abuse by Americans
2007-02-18.abstract: A former detainee said U.S. guards casually used stun guns and exposed prisoners to periods of heat and cold.

2007-02-19.title: Tiny Bones Found at India Hospital
2007-02-19.abstract: The police in central India discovered 396 small bones and bone fragments that they say could be the remains of newborn babies or fetuses.

2007-02-19.title: Australian Held at Guantánamo May Go Home
2007-02-19.abstract: David Hicks, who has been held at the American military base in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, for more than five years without trial, is to return to Australia by the end of the year.

2007-02-19.title: Top Israeli Police Official Resigns Amid Charges of Dereliction
2007-02-19.abstract: Moshe Karadi, whose term would have expired in August, said he was resigning immediately to “set a personal example.”

2007-02-19.title: Terror Officials See Al Qaeda Chiefs Regaining Power
2007-02-19.abstract: American officials say Al Qaeda has re-established significant control over its once battered worldwide terror network.

2007-02-19.title: Draft Law on Oil Money Moves to Iraqi Cabinet
2007-02-19.abstract: The first step has been taken toward approving legislation that would govern the development of Iraqi oil fields and the distribution of oil revenues.

2007-02-20.title: Iran’s President Defiant on Nuclear Program
2007-02-20.abstract: President Ahmadinejad said Iran will halt its uranium enrichment program only if Western powers do the same.

2007-02-20.title: Rape Charge Splits Iraqi Sunnis and Shiites
2007-02-20.abstract: The most wicked acts are spoken of openly and without reserve in Iraq. Rape is different.

2007-02-20.title: France: Lawmakers Amend Charter for Presidential Impeachment
2007-02-20.abstract: President Jacques Chirac convened both houses of Parliament for a joint session and won approval for a constitutional amendment that introduces an American-style impeachment procedure. Approval of measure, a pledge from Mr. Chirac’s 2002 campaign, was widely viewed as his last major political act before he hands over the presidency in May.

2007-02-20.title: Honoring a Westerner Who Preserved Japan’s Folk Tales
2007-02-20.abstract: Descriptions of Matsue, Japan by Lafcadio Hearn, the Irish-Greek author and muckraking journalist, put this medieval city on the map in the 1890s.

2007-02-20.title: Iran: Man Hanged for Role in Bus Bombing
2007-02-20.abstract: Iran hanged a man convicted of involvement in the bombing of a bus carrying members of the Revolutionary Guards on Wednesday, the ILNA news agency reported. Eleven people were killed in the blast in southeastern Zahedan. According to an official in the governor’s office, the man, Nassrollah Shanbeh Zehi, had come from Pakistan two days earlier, the news agency said.

2007-02-21.title: Afghan Suicide Bomber Wounds 6 Soldiers
2007-02-21.abstract: A suicide bomber disguised as a doctor blew himself up Tuesday morning at the opening of a hospital ward built by American forces in eastern Afghanistan.

2007-02-21.title: After the Mecca Accord, Clouded Horizons
2007-02-21.abstract: The Saudi-brokered pact between warring Palestinian factions largely torpedoed peace talks between the Israelis and the Palestinians.

2007-02-21.title: Tougher Tactics Deter Migrants at U.S. Border
2007-02-21.abstract: More agents, increased patrols and new walls and fences have made the border harder to cross.

2007-02-21.title: Israel: Police Say Tip Thwarts Suicide Attack
2007-02-21.abstract: Acting on a tip, the police arrested a Palestinian suspected of planning a suicide bombing in the Tel Aviv area, the authorities said. The police set up roadblocks around Tel Aviv and conducted an intensive search, arresting the Palestinian, identified as Omar Abu Rob, at an apartment in the suburb of Bat Yam. He led the police to the nearby city of Rishon Letzion, where they said he had hidden explosives for the attack. Several men suspected of helping of him were also arrested.

2007-02-21.title: 4 Salvadorans Killed in Way That Evokes ’80s Conflict
2007-02-21.abstract: Three Salvadoran lawmakers and their driver were shot and their bodies burned in what may be a sign that the ghosts of the bitter Central American civil wars of the 1980s still linger.

2007-02-22.title: Serbs Face Tough Choice as Kosovo Independence Looms
2007-02-22.abstract: The estimated 120,000 Serbs left in the majority-Albanian province, many of them natives, are wondering whether to go or to stay.

2007-02-22.title: Iraq Insurgents Employ Chlorine in Bomb Attacks
2007-02-22.abstract: At least three truck bombs in a month have used the gas, which burns the skin and can be fatal after only a few concentrated breaths.

2007-02-22.title: U.S. Sends Russia Message on Antimissile Plan
2007-02-22.abstract: Senior American officials tried today to allay Russian concerns about plans to install antimissile defenses in Eastern Europe, but they also said that NATO will not be deterred from continuing to enlarge.

2007-02-22.title: Italian Premier Resigns After Losing Foreign Policy Vote
2007-02-22.abstract: Italy’s fragile government snapped over internal divisions and skepticism about Europe’s role in fighting terrorism.

2007-02-22.title: Prince Harry of Britain to Serve in Iraq
2007-02-22.abstract: Harry, 22, is a junior officer in the upper-crust Blues and Royals regiment of the Household Cavalry.

2007-02-23.title: Prince Harry, 3rd in Line to Throne, to Fight in Iraq
2007-02-23.abstract: Prince Harry has long insisted that he would not countenance the idea of the soldiers under his command in an armored reconnaissance unit going without him.

2007-02-23.title: Circumcision’s Anti-AIDS Effect Found Greater Than First Thought
2007-02-23.abstract: Final data from two clinical trials in Africa suggest that circumcision reduces a man’s risk of contracting H.I.V. by as much as 65 percent — more than suspected when the two trials were stopped because the results were so clear.

2007-02-23.title: Palestinian Universities Dragged Into Factional Clashes
2007-02-23.abstract: Islamic and Al Azhar universities in Gaza, long the pride of Palestinians, have been consumed by the fighting between Hamas and Fatah.

2007-02-23.title: After the Fall, Italy Considers Its Options
2007-02-23.abstract: A day after Prime Minister Romano Prodi resigned, work began to see if the government could be put back together — or if Italy should return to the polls.

2007-02-23.title: Britain: Letter Bombing Suspect Charged
2007-02-23.abstract: The police said they had charged a 27-year-old school janitor, Miles Cooper, with explosives and assault offenses in the string of letter bombs sent to several companies this year that wounded seven people.

2007-02-24.title: South Africa: 8 Accused of Plot Found Not Guilty
2007-02-24.abstract: Eight South African men accused of joining in a March 2004 attempt to overthrow the dictatorship of Equatorial Guinea were found not guilty by a South African court, which ruled that a number of them thought that their efforts had been endorsed by South Africa’s government. Others apparently believed that they were en route to guard a diamond mine in Congo, the judge, Magistrate Peet Johnson, said. He called it “eerie” that the men had been brought to trial, saying South African prosecutors’ own witnesses contradicted the government’s case. The men were seized after landing in Harare, Zimbabwe, in March 2004, apparently to refuel and pick up weapons.

2007-02-24.title: U.S. Seizes Son of a Top Shiite, Stirring Uproar
2007-02-24.abstract: The detention of Abdul Aziz al-Hakim's eldest son agitated one of Iraq’s most formidable political movements.

2007-02-24.title: Blast That Killed U.S. Diplomat Tied to Qaeda
2007-02-24.abstract: Pakistan presented evidence that the bombing last March was masterminded by an operative of Al Qaeda.

2007-02-24.title: Canadian Court Limits Detention in Terror Cases
2007-02-24.abstract: The decision reflected striking differences from the current legal climate in the United States.

2007-02-24.title: Canada: Native Canadians File Rights Complaint Over Child Welfare
2007-02-24.abstract: A national organization representing Native Canadians, the Assembly of First Nations, filed a complaint with the Canadian Human Rights Commission over what the group says is a lack of financing for child welfare programs that has put thousands of indigenous children at risk. “It is really sad we have been pushed to do this,” said Phil Fontaine the group’s leader.

2007-02-25.title: Iran Defiant Before 6-Power Meeting on Sanctions
2007-02-25.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad said today that his country’s nuclear program had no “reverse gear.”

2007-02-25.title: Prodi Gets a Second Chance to Lead Italy
2007-02-25.abstract: Allies and enemies worried that the premier’s coalition would remain divided and vulnerable to another collapse.

2007-02-25.title: Japan Launches Its 4th Spy Satellite
2007-02-25.abstract: The satellite launch moves Japan away from its traditional reliance on the United States for intelligence.

2007-02-25.title: Iraq Rebel Cleric Reins In Militia; Motives at Issue
2007-02-25.abstract: Moktada al-Sadr seems to be cooperating with the latest effort to pacify Baghdad, but why he is doing so remains unclear.

2007-02-25.title: Lives Entwined by War Enter a Long, Arduous Chapter: Recovery
2007-02-25.abstract: Petty Officer Third Class Dustin E. Kirby and Marine Lance Cpl. Colin Smith were thrown together on a battlefield in Iraq.

2007-02-26.title: Bush to Warn Pakistan to Act on Terror
2007-02-26.abstract: President Bush will send a tough message to President Pervez Musharraf that U.S. aid could be cut unless he becomes more aggressive in hunting Al Qaeda leaders.

2007-02-26.title: U.N. Court Issues Ruling on Bosnia Genocide
2007-02-26.abstract: The court cleared Serbia of intent to commit genocide, but said it had failed to prevent the Srebrenica massacre.

2007-02-26.title: Thais Struggle With Violent Insurgency
2007-02-26.abstract: Since a new government policy of conciliation toward Muslim separatists, violence has grown worse in southern Thailand.

2007-02-26.title: Cheney Warns Pakistan to Act on Terrorism
2007-02-26.abstract: The vice president delivered  a tough message to Gen. Pervez Musharraf that U.S. aid could be cut unless he becomes more aggressive in hunting Al Qaeda leaders.

2007-02-26.title: 2-Year Study of Polar Changes Set to Begin
2007-02-26.abstract: The effort by scientists from more than 60 countries aims to understand the dynamics of life at the ends of the earth.

2007-02-27.title: Palestinian Killed in Israeli Sweep of West Bank City
2007-02-27.abstract: Israeli troops searched for militants and weapons in the West Bank for a third day, going door to door and clashing with gunmen and stone-throwing youths.

2007-02-27.title: British Court Affirms Order to Deport Cleric to Jordan
2007-02-27.abstract: The ruling set the stage for the deportation of other foreign terrorism suspects in Britain to countries with poor human rights records.

2007-02-27.title: Bombing at Iraqi Ministry Wounds 2 Top Officials
2007-02-27.abstract: The attack was the most serious breach of an Iraqi government building since November.

2007-02-27.title: Iraqis Reach an Accord on Oil Revenues
2007-02-27.abstract: The move marked a major agreement among the country’s political blocs on a highly divisive issue.

2007-02-27.title: Diplomats to Begin Drafting New U.N. Sanctions on Iran
2007-02-27.abstract: The Security Council agreed to begin working on a new resolution after reports last week that Iran was expanding its efforts to enrich uranium, in continuing defiance of the United Nations.

2007-02-28.title: Officials Welcome U.S. Move to Join Iran in Talks
2007-02-28.abstract: Lee H. Hamilton, co-chairman of the Iraq Study Group, noted that it reflected a “huge change” in the White House’s approach.

2007-02-28.title: Italy: Island Volcano Spews Lava
2007-02-28.abstract: Eruptions from a volcano on Stromboli have strengthened to the point that emergency procedures to protect the island’s 750 residents have been put in place. Civil defense officials say the measures are, at this point, precautionary. But lava has begun flowing into the sea, and two new craters have appeared at the summit of the volcano, active for the last 2,000 years. An eruption in 2002 caused a small tsunami and closed the island, north of Sicily, to tourists for months.

2007-02-28.title: Senegal: Incumbent on Course for Second Term
2007-02-28.abstract: Preliminary results of the presidential election on Sunday announced on public and private radio appeared to give the octogenarian incumbent, Abdoulaye Wade, a solid majority that would allow him to avoid a runoff. A runoff would be required if none of the 15 candidates won more than 50 percent. A spokeswoman for the candidate of the Socialist Party, which governed for four decades until Mr. Wade’s election in 2000, said it planned to contest the results.

2007-02-28.title: China: Premier Rules Out Democratic Reform in Near Future
2007-02-28.abstract: In comments published by state news media less than a week before the annual parliamentary session is to open, Prime Minister Wen Jiabao ruled out any plan for more democracy in the near future as the country focuses on economic and social development. He said that while China was committed to political reform, progress toward democracy would be difficult because the economic, political and legal systems were still immature.

2007-02-28.title: Divided Congress Prepares to Debate Financing and Strategy for Iraq War
2007-02-28.abstract: Members of Congress returned to Capitol Hill searching for a way to translate a tide of public sentiment against the war into legislation.
